other_findings: Pre-pro-B cells are the most primitive B-lineage committed progenitors in fetal skin
Correspond to Hardy fraction A in the standard B cell developmental framework
Express CD34 and lack fully rearranged immunoglobulin genes
In fetal liver, pre-pro-B clusters express CD34, SPINK2, and IGLL1 (Popescu et al., 2019)
IL-7 receptor signaling is necessary for the transition from pre-pro-B to pro-B stage in adult B cell development
evidence_quotes: primitive 'pre pro-B' clusters expressing CD34, SPINK2, and IGLL1 to 'pro-B' and 'pre-B' clusters with increasing expression of B cell transcripts MS4A1, CD79B, DNTT and HLA-DRA and reducing expression of JCHAIN and LTB
